sourceScope.primarySourceFile: AG 19 - Guideline Concerning 1980 Commissioners' Standard Ordinary Mortality Table With 10-Year Select Mortality Factors.pdf
sourceScope.sourceTitle: AG 19 - Guideline Concerning 1980 Commissioners' Standard Ordinary Mortality Table With 10-Year Select Mortality Factors

observedSectionWindows.note: One-page mortality-table guideline for the 1980 CSO select mortality factors.

boundaries: Keep planning focused on this single AG 19 PDF.
Do not widen into AG 20 or later guideline files.
Keep the active guideline posture explicit in every batch artifact.
Treat the guideline as review-only mortality-table guidance, not learner-facing policy text.
Keep any PDF text-extraction noise visible as a caveat, not a promotion signal.
Keep the batch source-bound and review-only.
exclusions: No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No other guideline files in the same wave.

topicMap.label: 1980 CSO mortality table with ten-year select mortality factors interpretation
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep this slice within page 1 and do not absorb later guideline files.

proposedBatches.title: One-page AG 19 mortality-table guideline

pageTarget.notes: Capture the full one-page guideline as a single review-only slice and stop at the page boundary.

proposedBatches.rationale: Capture the full one-page guideline as a single review-only slice and stop at the page boundary.

reviewStandards.regulatoryRequirement: Treat explicit must, shall, should, or required language as the requirement; preserve the citation and source context.
reviewStandards.definitionOrTerminology: Treat 1980 CSO mortality table, Ten-Year Select Mortality Factors, and alternative select-factor phrasing as terminology when the text explains rather than directs.
reviewStandards.calculationStructure: Treat the mortality-table and select-factor framing as calculation structure until the text clearly instructs a separate duty.
reviewStandards.formulaContext: Treat table-factor references and mortality-table wording as context until the text clearly instructs a calculation step.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceMapping: Treat references to the Standard Valuation Law, the Standard Nonforfeiture Law, or Society of Actuaries materials as mapping notes unless operational text is included.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat the title line and framing sentence as background content unless they impose a duty.
reviewStandards.boundaryControlWindow: Treat the one-page slice as a review-only window that should not be merged forward.
reviewStandards.requiresHumanInterpretation: Treat the indexing choice and the OCR / encoded text layer as requiring human actuarial interpretation unless the text is explicit.
reviewStandards.sourceQualityCaveat: Treat text-layer noise as review-only caveats and do not widen the batch because of them.

appReady: export is sanitized, stable, and versioned
ragReady: review status no longer requires human disposition

currentChecksNeeded: Plan file exists and parses as JSON.
The AG 19 plan markdown exists and carries the expected headings.
Every proposed batch is review-only by default.
The plan keeps the scope to one page only.
The source posture is explicitly active.
Each planned batch has a matching batch definition.
The AG 19 batch definition file stays synchronized with the plan.
futureChecksSuggested: One-page guideline checks for future short guideline files.
Mortality-table context and cross-reference mapping checks.
No learner-facing, app-ready, or RAG-ready promotion checks.
Text-layer quality caveat checks once extraction starts.
scriptImplications: scripts/ag19-batch-definitions.mjs should stay synchronized with the planned batch-097 slice when extraction is authorized.
scripts/batch-definitions.mjs should import the AG 19 definition file so the shared runner can find the planned batch ID.
scripts/validate-scaffold.mjs should verify that the AG 19 plan file exists, parses cleanly, and preserves the review-only posture.
The shared runner should continue to honor the same stop conditions used for the VM-20 batches.

notes: Controlled AG 19 planning artifact. Planned batch 097 remains review-only by default and is not an extraction run.
